rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE SHIRLEY OUTIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/1/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: An amendment was just filed on 5/23/22. (No courtesy copy has been received yet by the assigned examiner as of 6/3/22.) A cursory review of the pleading online (as a ONE-TIME courtesy in hopes of not having to continue the hearing again) shows that it is incomplete: no exhibits appear to be attached to it. (In any event, an "amendment in supplement" to a pleading is still an amendment and not a supplement, which are two different kinds of pleadings, as determined by the legislature [CRC 7.3], and attaching a printout of the tentative ruling is unnecessary - issues should simply be addressed in an amendment or supplement without reference to Examiner's Notes or tentative rulings.) Any pleading filed to supplement or amend an accounting by a fiduciary must be signed and verified by the fiduciary. [CRC 7.103(c).] An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Courtesy copies of the amendment, proof of service of the amendment, and any other papers filed to address the issues posted in the tentative ruling for 3/14/22 must be received by the assigned Examiner by NO LATER THAN 7/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: **TRANSFERRED TO CONTRA COSTA** THE ANNA MARIA MERCADO 2009 IRREVOCABLE SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #6, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/3/22. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: No receipts were attached to the amended accounting petition, which should support the $1,039.66 in expenses Carmen Mercado has reportedly paid from her own funds. (See subparagraph a of paragraph 8.) Schedule C shows a disbursement of $4,200 to the prior attorney, Kevin Urbatsch, based on an order dated 8/28/20, but that order authorized just $3,579.00 in attorney fees and $500 for costs and also ordered Mr. Urbatsch to return $696.50 for overpaid fees and costs. It appears he was overpaid another $121.00. Has he refunded the trust $696.50? Liabilities are not credits reflected on the summary of account; they should just be shown on an additional schedule. The liabilities schedule (Schedule L) shows the full amount of $2,300, although $500 was reportedly already paid. The attorney time sheets have several entries under "Nonbillable Hours" that appear to have been billed $350.00 each anyway. Several entries (checking on the status of hearings, filing papers, sending documents to the court, etc.) appear to be services a paralegal or secretary could provide. Most bonding companies will not bond for less than $10,000. Have the co-trustees confirmed that their bond could be reduced to less than $10,000? They might wish to amend their amended petition to seek exoneration of their bond. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/25/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSE ALFREDO ZERMENO II
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/18/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 7/05/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE ONG FAMILY TRUST DATED MAY 28, 2002
rulings.Calendar Matter: General Demurrer
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is entered by Judge Ross C. Moody. This matter was initiated through the filing of a Verified Petition for 1) Order for Accounting; 2) Order for Damages, Attorney's Fees and Costs for Failure to Timely Provide Notice of Trust on October 7, 2021. On March 9, 2022, Respondents Co-Trustees Michael J. Ong and Kevin J. Ong filed Respondent's General Demurrer to the initial verified petition. On April 20, 2022, while the General Demurrer was pending, Petitioner Joseph P. Ong filed a First Amended Petition for 1) Order for Accounting; 2) Order to Quiet Title and Recover Trust Assets; 3) Order for Suspension and Removal of Trustee and Successor Trustee and Appointment of New Trustee; 4) Order Voiding Trust Due to Undue Influence. Because Respondents' General Demurrer does not address First Amended Petition, it is OVERULED as moot. Petitioner shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above, prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on June 7, 2022. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925#, participant ID 0#, and passcode 722809#. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE SARANTITIS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Petitioner did not check the required boxes on page 3 of the amended petition under #5.a and b. Petitioner must select box (3) or (4) and (7) or (8) and check on 5.b. Decedent was or was not survived by a stepchild or foster child. 2) Petitioner must file proof of publication for the amended petition. [CRC 7.53 (a)] 3) Petitioner must file and give notice of the amended petition. [CRC 7.53 (a)] 4) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/27/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GEORGE CHRISTOPHER STOLL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 8/1/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately, conventionally, and confidentially. When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Who were the decedent's parents and when did they die? Item 3a of the petition is incomplete. (Item 3g(1)(c) should have been checked, as the sister had higher priority and essentially nominated Petitioner. Item 3g(2) of the petition should not be completed when there is a will.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/21/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
